algorithms.subtitle: Top 100 high-frequency algorithm problems to help with coding interviews
algorithms.complexityDesc1: m and n are the lengths of the two linked lists, at most traversing m + n nodes
algorithms.complexityDesc2: Only two pointer variables are used, space complexity is constant
algorithms.complexityDesc3: n is the number of nodes in the binary tree, worst case needs to traverse all nodes
algorithms.complexityDesc4: h is the height of the binary tree, the depth of the recursive call stack is the height of the tree

intersection.title: Intersection of Two Linked Lists
intersection.description: Given the heads of two singly linked-lists headA and headB, return the node at which the two lists intersect. If the two linked lists have no intersection at all, return null.

steps.init: Initialize: Pointer A points to head of list A (value {valA}), Pointer B points to head of list B (value {valB})
steps.switchA: Pointer A reaches end of list A, switch to head of list B (value {val})
steps.switchB: Pointer B reaches end of list B, switch to head of list A (value {val})
steps.move: Pointer A={valA} ({posA}), B={valB} ({posB}), move one step simultaneously
steps.found: 🎯 Found intersection node! Value is {val}, located at list A[{indexA}] and list B[{indexB}]
steps.notFound: The two linked lists have no intersection (both pointers become null)

lca.title: Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Tree
lca.description: Given a binary tree, find the lowest common ancestor (LCA) of two given nodes in the tree. According to the definition of LCA on Wikipedia: 'The lowest common ancestor is defined between two nodes p and q as the lowest node in T that has both p and q as descendants (where we allow a node to be a descendant of itself).'

steps.start: Start finding the lowest common ancestor of nodes {p} and {q}
steps.isTarget: Node {val} is one of the target nodes, return this node
steps.foundLCA: Both left and right subtrees of node {val} contain target nodes, {val} is the lowest common ancestor!
steps.returnLeft: Left subtree found target node, return node {val} upward
steps.returnRight: Right subtree found target node, return node {val} upward
steps.notFound: Target nodes not found in subtrees of node {val}, return null

content.opensource-projects:
# Open Source Projects Navigation

This article organizes some representative repositories on my GitHub by "learning path + engineering value" to help you quickly find the direction you care about.

> GitHub Profile: [@xianqiuyu](https://github.com/xianqiuyu)

## 1) Source Implementation (Building from Scratch, Understanding Principles)

* **mini-vue3**: Implement Vue3 core reactivity, computed, and virtual DOM renderer from scratch  
  - Recommended for: Those who want to master Vue3 reactivity/renderer  
  - Link: [mini-vue3](https://github.com/xianqiuyu/mini-vue3)
* **mini-react**: Implement a mini version of React Fiber / Hooks / Scheduler  
  - Recommended for: Those who want to understand React16+ core  
  - Link: [mini-react](https://github.com/xianqiuyu/mini-react)

## 2) Source Reading (Systematic Notes, Building Cognitive Maps)

* **vue3-source-analysis**: Vue3 source code reading notes (Reactivity / Virtual DOM / Compiler / Component System)  
  - Link: [vue3-source-analysis](https://github.com/xianqiuyu/vue3-source-analysis)
* **react-source-analysis**: React source code reading notes (Fiber / Reconciliation / Hooks / Scheduler)  
  - Link: [react-source-analysis](https://github.com/xianqiuyu/react-source-analysis)

## 3) Component Library (Engineering and Design System Implementation)

* **vue3-component-lib**: Vue3 component library referencing Element Plus (with overview/Playground)  
  - Link: [vue3-component-lib](https://github.com/xianqiuyu/vue3-component-lib)
* **react-component-lib**: React component library referencing Ant Design (each component with demo)  
  - Link: [react-component-lib](https://github.com/xianqiuyu/react-component-lib)

## 4) Admin Template (Routing/Permissions/Engineering Skeleton)

* **vue3-admin-template**: Streamlined admin template with Vue3 + Router + Pinia + Axios (login authentication/role routing)  
  - Link: [vue3-admin-template](https://github.com/xianqiuyu/vue3-admin-template)
* **react-admin-template**: Admin template with React Router + Zustand + Axios (aligned with Vue version model)  
  - Link: [react-admin-template](https://github.com/xianqiuyu/react-admin-template)

## 5) Engineering / Build (Bundler Principles)

* **mini-webpack**: Mini Webpack with dependency resolution + module bundling + runtime require  
  - Link: [mini-webpack](https://github.com/xianqiuyu/mini-webpack)
* **mini-bundler**: Mini bundler implementing AST + dependency graph + Loader/Plugin + Babel process  
  - Link: [mini-bundler](https://github.com/xianqiuyu/mini-bundler)

## 6) Hooks / Tools (Highly Reusable Business Abstractions)

* **react-hooks-collection**: Business-level Hooks collection (useRequest / useVirtualList / useWebSocket, etc.)  
  - Link: [react-hooks-collection](https://github.com/xianqiuyu/react-hooks-collection)

## Afterword

I will break down the "design trade-offs, pitfalls, and reusable patterns" behind each project into a series of articles, and welcome you to raise Issues on GitHub for discussion.
